

“Luck creates Monsters.”

“What creates a monster?”

Fem presenting black man. 

“Why do I have to change myself to meet y’all’s expectations of what sexy looks like? Always wears the same style sequined top in different colors. Deep rich james earl jones voice. 

All characters have their one core truth and all emotions and dialogue paths stem from that.  This can be a secret guilt or a positive idea. 

For example, the church ladies core truth is “I want to be with my dead son again.  Jesus is the only way that’s possible. “ so that when she asks if you have a home at church yet? It’s coming from a desperate need to be believed and validated in the theory that the more believers are the more likely it is to be true. She embraces newcomers but shuns apostates. 

Game lore

Lake look, but don’t touch. 

Poetry for a fever break

Ditchweed

Ponders the way out 

yonder as he picks his nose

thinking about heading back

broke and broken to Maine

And he explains he’s got family

there even though you know

he came all the way out here 

to steer clear of them. 

Maybe Mendocito County 

could be better. Buddy 

sent him a letter

said he could get him in 

on the ground 

floor of some top shelf ditch weed. 

*Feral cannabis: often strong and tall and and able to withstand the elements.* 

He chuckles at the chinkle of 

the styrofoam big gulp cup

filled to the brim in front of him.

“Sorry bro” he says 

returning his finger back to earth 

and crash landing the wreckage

 on his shirt. 

“Baja blast off. Used to be

 my favorite too. T-bell 

exclusivo back in the day. 

Seventy nine cents.”

The dollar bill uncrinkles 

from the hand of a regular.

Sleds it across the 

Greasy glass. Let’s it pass up

Up and away. Silent, as his 

fingers rain down across the cup

before turning his palm up 

and grasping for change. 

“Yeah. The Doc said 

I gotta stop. The Blood 

pressure runs high with my family. 

Got heart problems.”

The deal cemented in a slurp. 

“Hah. Don’t we all?”

Our characters core truth: he just wants the pain to go away. Through players choices in the story we find out what that pain is. 

Open with quote:

I think that there's probably a revelatory experience awaiting everyone, that has to do with finding out who and what you really are.

I think that when that occurs, if it occurs, you reach Nirvana-Heaven. And the degree to which you don't reach that place of realization--you're in eternal Hell.

\--James Toback, The Big Bang

Screenwriter (Oscar nominated), sexual predator (alleged). 

Cut to a slowly sinking grapefruit with a needle sticking out. And as it sinks the sun rises to take its place in the middle third “dream extreme” appears on the waters surface. 

Cut to 

The bottom beach. No pain but for the constant ringing in your ear. 

Story text, brief character discovery through remembering dream fragments

Surrealist dream-like version of the final choice to burn the last location with a cigarette.

The constant ringing continues.

Open up with the first location.

\==The Bandlands==

Location \#1: Abandoned Golf Course

Location \#2: Neighbor Street \+ Crackhouse

Location \#3: Bar & Chinese Food Restaurant Intersection

Location \#4 Gas Station

\-Bar \= Hell’s Kitchen Design \= Bar and Grill Design “Yardhouse for Shitheads”

\==LOCATION \#1==

UI for main game begins.

We see LOCATION, DIALOG AREA, and when the player answers the phone, PHONE UI appears. 

You see app notification for Therapy App (FEELZ)

![][image1]

**Setting:** Lake Ulysses, a rundown town with a troubled past and a pretentious revitalization effort.

**Genre:** Point-and-click adventure game with some RPG elements.

**Story:** The player character wakes up from a dream and interacts with a therapist app. The core mechanic involves navigating the "wheel of emotions" to connect with NPCs and progress the story.

**Gameplay:**

* Explore different locations in Lake Ulysses.  
* Choose three emotions from the "wheel of emotions" to represent your current state.  
* Interact with NPCs and select an emotion to use in response to their facial expressions.  
* Choices affect the story and potentially unlock new parts of the emotion wheel.

**Additional Notes:**

* **Art Direction:** The game will have a retro Macintosh IIE/Macintosh Classic 1-bit Graphics, and underground comics with limited color palette based on the therapy app: FEELZ WHEEL.  
* The NPCs will each have a core emotion and three associated emotions.  
* The player's goal is to progress the story, however they have the option to go their own path based on their own emotional capacity determined by the npc, location, and available emotions in the FEELZ app.   
  * Player will be able to go a path that doesn’t resolve the NPC’s issue, but might miss on critical information that will divert the outcomes of the later part of the story.  
  * No matter the choice however, the story progresses and the player’s experience centers.

* **Worldbuilding:** Flesh out the lore and history of Lake Elsinore and its inhabitants.  
* **Character Design:** Create unique and memorable NPCs with distinct personalities and emotions.  
* **Storytelling:** Craft a compelling narrative with branching paths based on player choices.  
* **Dialogue Writing:** Write engaging dialogue that reflects the characters' emotions.

**Key Changes for the Emotion Wheel Evolution:**

* **Visual Progression:** The Emotion Wheel now visually progresses heavily pixelated to clearly pixelated mirroring the unlocking of emotions and the player's emotional growth for additional choices in the dialog battles.

* **Emphasis on Potential:** The final narration highlights the "potential for more," suggesting that the player can unlock all emotions and achieve a greater understanding of themselves, the community, and solutions for its problems. 

* **Reinforced Connection to Plot:** The wheel's evolution during the therapist interaction links the player's emotional journey directly to the plot, suggesting that confronting the conspiracy will require them to fully embrace their emotions.

  ---

**Trailer Structure**

**Opening \- Manufactured Nostalgia**

* **Visual:** Open on a pixelated, black and white panorama of Lake Ulysses. The scene depicts a "historic" downtown area, but it's clearly a recent construction trying to evoke a bygone era. Buildings are blocky and simplistic, with exaggerated details like oversized signs and perfectly manicured (pixelated) trees. The lake itself is a flat gray surface reflecting the gray sky.  
* **Audio:** A simple, upbeat chiptune melody plays, but it has a tinny, artificial quality, like a cheap music box.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box, blocky font) ***"Welcome to Lake Ulysses. Where there’s a high price to make history\!"***  
* 

**Waking Up \- Monochrome Hangover**

* **Visual:** Hard cut to the player character, in black and white pixel art, sprawled on the side of a cracked road overlooking the lake.  
* **Audio:** The chiptune cuts abruptly, replaced by a low, distorted bass note and a muffled, echoing sound of lapping water.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box) "The hangover of a forgotten dream."

**Therapy App \- A Colored Plea**

* **Visual:** The player’s hand pulls out their phone transitioning to the PHONE appearing on the UI. The app interface is black and white, but the app's icon is a color.. The app's name is "Feelz."  
* **Audio:** A simple, 8-bit jingle plays when the app is opened, with the app icon accompanied by a high-pitched chime.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box) "Feelz. “Choose your Peace"

**Emotion Wheel \- The Spectrum of Memory**

* **Visual:** The Emotion Wheel appears: a black and white circle divided into segments, each representing a core emotion with a distinct color: Joy (yellow), Trust (light blue), Fear (dark blue), Surprise (purple), Sadness (gray – a slightly lighter shade than the background), Disgust (green), Anger (red), Anticipation (orange).   
  * As the cursor moves, each segment glows with its respective color. The fragmented memories are now more distinct, showing pixelated images of specific locations in Lake Ulysses, both old and new.  
* **Audio:** Each emotion has a unique 8-bit sound and a corresponding colored flash.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box) "Eight colors. Eight fragments of his story…in this case YOURS\!

**Emotion Wheel \- A Single Pixel of Feeling**

* **Visual:** The Emotion Wheel first appears as a simple, black and white circle. Only *one* segment is colored: Fear (dark blue). It's a single pixel within the segment, barely visible. The fragmented memories are still monochrome and fleeting.  
* **Audio:** Only the Fear sound effect (a low, echoing tone) plays.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box) "One pixel. One feeling. The beginning."

**Emotion Selection \- The First Step**

* **Visual:** The player selects Fear. The single blue pixel expands to fill the entire Fear segment of the wheel. As they select Sadness (gray) and Guilt (brown), a single pixel of each color appears within their respective segments. The silhouette of the burning building is still monochrome with colored flames.  
* **Audio:** The selected emotion sounds play, followed by the distorted combination.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box) "More colors emerge. The past grows clearer."

**Therapist Interaction \- The Xtreme Offer & Emotional Evolution**

* **Visual:** As the shadowy figure speaks and the offer is made, the Emotion Wheel in the background *transforms*. It shifts from highly pixelated, to a clear pixelated version.   
  * The selected emotions now have more defined shapes and shading within their segments.   
  * As the narrator says "DreamXtreme," the wheel *again* transforms, this time to a more complex wheel revealing more emotional states.  
  * This visual evolution happens quickly, almost like a time-lapse, showing the player's emotional capacity expanding.   
* **Audio:** The distorted chiptune intensifies, but now with added layers of sound, becoming richer and more complex as the wheel evolves. The digitized whisper of "DreamXtreme" is also clearer and more menacing.  
* **Narrator:** (Text box, colored text, which changes color as the wheel evolves)."  
* Final visual: The game title, "DreamXtreme," appears with the teal pixel in the "X" now expanded into a small, but distinct shape. The logo is the burning building with colored flames and the "X," but now with slightly more detail than before.

Here's a possible mapping of the 8 core emotions to musical notes, keeping in mind that this is subjective and can be adapted to your specific needs:

* **Joy:** C Major \- A bright, uplifting, and consonant sound.  
* **Trust:** G Major \- A stable and reassuring sound, often used to create a sense of peace.  
* **Fear:** D Minor \- A tense and unsettling sound, often used to create suspense or anxiety.  
* **Surprise:** A sudden, dissonant chord or a quick change in tempo or dynamics.  
* **Sadness:** A Minor \- A melancholic and introspective sound.  
* **Disgust:** A low, dissonant note or a jarring sound effect.  
* **Anger:** A sharp, loud, and dissonant chord, potentially in a minor key.  
* **Anticipation:** A sustained note or a rising melody that creates a sense of expectation.

**NPCS**

1. SAMUN \- Junkie (Meth), actually was a brilliant kid but came from an abusive immigrant family. Joined the military, got worse. Drug habit returned and because me homeless because of habit the layers of clothes act like layers of army for him. Always upbeat and cocky..

2. DEBORAH \- Religious MLM Karen, “You got a home church?”, guilty of the death of her son and is religiously obsessed so that she will be reunited with her son in the afterlife. (Passively Suicidal), wants to get to her son as soon as possible. (Son died of covid, denier and believed that faith was enough, but it killed her son since she was an anti-vaxxer), lies to themselves that it's not their fault.

3. RWANDA \- Queer Femme Afro-Latino Struggling Artist (James Earl Jones Voice) Struggling artist, is constantly being harassed and put down by their community and don’t have many or any friends. Very smart, neurodivergent, ADHD, and got zingers. Comfortable with who they are. Constantly on defense because of their environment. 

4.   
   RICHARD “RICK” WHITEHEAD \- Machismo Biker Gang Member, Loyalty Obsessed, Bandana (Combination of JTC’s “[The Sissy](https://www.chick.com/products/tract?stk=0086)” and “[Bad Bob](https://www.chick.com/products/tract?stk=21)” He’s a hells angel Nepo grandbaby, closeted homophobe, loves muscular men because of parental issues. Lots to prove. 

First screen,Bottoms Badlands, just player

Second Screen, Condo/Crackhouse,  Karen

Third screen: Rwanda.  Chinese restaurant

Fourth Screen: Samun. Gas Station

Fifth screen: Rick, Biker Bar & Grill
